COOKIES_PAGE_P1: Cookies are small files saved on your phone, tablet or computer when you visit a website.

COOKIES_PAGE_P3: With your permission, we use Google Analytics to collect data about how you use this service. This information helps us to improve our service.
COOKIES_PAGE_P4: Google is not allowed to use or share our analytics data with anyaone.

COOKIE_PEGAODXDI_DESCRIPTION: This is used to uniquely identify a device that has interacted with HMRC services and is used for internal security auditing.
PEGA_SESSION_COOKIE_DESCRIPTION: Keeps your session on a single server so the service is more stable.
PEGA_SESSION_COOKIE_CORS_DESCRIPTION: Supports secure connections, while remembering your preferences across different parts of the site. It does this without tracking you.
COOKIE_AKA_A2_DESCRIPTION: Used to speed up content delivery in the service through adaptive accelration.

IN_THE_VAST_MAJORITY_OF_CASES_YOUR_TAX_C: In the vast majority of cases, your tax code will stay the same if you’re receiving less than:

IF_YOUR_NEW_ESTIMATED_TAXABLE_INCOME: If your new estimated taxable income does not change your tax code, you will not receive any further messages.
UPDATE_YOUR_ESTIMATED_TAXABLE_INCOME: Update your estimated taxable income if you are going to claim employment expenses or declare employment benefits or other income, to make sure you’re paying the correct amount of tax.
IF_YOU_YOUR_PARTNER_ARE_CLAIMING_CHILD_B: If you or your partner are claiming Child Benefit and your new overall individual estimated income is more than £60,000, you may be liable for High Income Child Benefit Charge.

BR: All of your income from this employment or pension will be taxed at 20%.
D0: All of your income from this employment or pension will be taxed at 40%.
D1: All of your income from this employment or pension will be taxed at 45%.
SBR: All of your income from this employment or pension will be taxed at 20%, the basic rate in Scotland.
SD0: All of your income from this employment or pension will be taxed at 21%, the intermediate rate in Scotland.
SD1: All of your income from this employment or pension will be taxed at 42%, the higher rate in Scotland.
SD2: All of your income from this employment or pension will be taxed at 45%, the advanced rate in Scotland.
SD3: All of your income from this employment or pension will be taxed at 48%, the top rate in Scotland.
CBR: All of your income from this employment or pension will be  taxed at 20%, the basic rate in Wales.
CD0: All of your income from this employment or pension will be  taxed at 40%, the higher rate in Wales.
CD1: All of your income from this employment or pension will be  taxed at 45%, the additional rate in Wales.
NT: Your income from this employment or pension will not be taxed.
BR_WEEK1_MONTH1: All of your income from this employment or pension will be taxed at 20%.
D0_WEEK1_MONTH1: All of your income from this employment or pension will be taxed at 40%.
D1_WEEK1_MONTH1: All of your income from this employment or pension will be taxed at 45%.
SBR_WEEK1_MONTH1: All of your income from this employment or pension will be taxed at 20%, the basic rate in Scotland.
SD0_WEEK1_MONTH1: All of your income from this employment or pension will be taxed at 21%, the intermediate rate in Scotland.
SD1_WEEK1_MONTH1: All of your income from this employment or pension will be taxed at 42%, the higher rate in Scotland.
SD2_WEEK1_MONTH1: All of your income from this employment or pension will be taxed at 45%, the advanced rate in Scotland.
SD3_WEEK1_MONTH1: All of your income from this employment or pension will be taxed at 48%, the top rate in Scotland.
CBR_WEEK1_MONTH1: All of your income from this employment or pension will be  taxed at 20%, the basic rate in Wales.
CD0_WEEK1_MONTH1: All of your income from this employment or pension will be  taxed at 40%, the higher rate in Wales.
CD1_WEEK1_MONTH1: All of your income from this employment or pension will be  taxed at 45%, the additional rate in Wales.
NT_WEEK1_MONTH1: Your income from this employment or pension will not be taxed.
VIEW_UPDATE_INFORMATION: View the list of information about your other income, allowances, employment benefits and deductions
OTHER_INCOMES_IABD: Other income, allowances, employment benefits and deductions
NO_INCOME_FROM_OTHER: You have no income from other sources.
LIST_SHOWS_ALL_PAY_AS_YOU_EARN: This list shows all of the Pay As You Earn (PAYE) information about your income from other sources, allowances, employment benefits and deductions.
AMOUNT_DO_NOT_ACCOUNT_PERSONAL_RELIEFS: These amounts do not account for the personal reliefs and allowances you may be entitled to. We apply those adjustments separately when calculating your tax code so the amounts in your tax code may be different from what is shown here.
NO_INCOME_OTHER_SOURCE: You have no income from other sources, allowances, employment benefits or deductions.
YOU_DO_NOT_DO_ANYTHING: You do not have to do anything.
EXPLAINER_CAT_A_BODY_INC: This change will increase the amount you can earn this tax year before paying tax.
EXPLAINER_CAT_A_BODY: If we need to, we will update your tax code to make sure you are paying the correct amount of tax.
EXPLAINER_CAT_A_BODY_DEC: This change will decrease the amount you can earn this tax year before paying tax.

COOKIE_BANNER.P2: We’d also like to use analytics cookies so we can understand how you use the  service and make improvements.

WE_HAVE_SENT: We have sent this to your
THIS_MAY_AFFECT: This may affect the amount of tax you pay when your
STARTS_TO_USE_TAX: starts to use this tax code.
THIS_IS_FOR_YOUR_INFO: This is for your information only. You do not have to do anything.
HOW_TAX_CODE_CALCULATED: How your tax-free amount is calculated
YOUR_TAX_FREE_AMOUNT_PENSION: Your tax-free amount for this pension is
YOUR_TAX_FREE_AMOUNT_EMPLOYMENT: Your tax-free amount for this employment is
UPDATE_REMOVE_INFO_ABOUT_YOU: update or remove the information we have about you
IF_YOU_THINK_INCORRECT: If you think this is incorrect, you can
CHANGES_TO_YOUR_TAX_CODE_AMOUNT: Changes to your tax code and tax-free amount
YOUR_INCOME_PENSION_TAXED_SCOTLAND_RATES: Your income or pension is taxed using the rates in Scotland
YOUR_INCOME_TAXED_WALES_RATES: Your income or pension is taxed using the rates in Wales
YOUR_INCOME_NOT_TAXED_WORTH_MORE_THAN_TA: You have income that is not being taxed another way and it’s worth more than your tax-free allowance
YOU_ENTITLED_STANDARD_TAX_FREE_PERSONAL_: You're entitled to the standard tax-free Personal Allowance
MARRAIGE_ALLOWANCE_YOU_RECEIVED_TEN_PERC: Marriage Allowance: you’ve received a transfer of 10% of your partner’s Personal Allowance
MARRAIGE_ALLOWANCE_YOU_TRANSFERED_TEN_PE: Marriage Allowance: you’ve transferred 10% of your Personal Allowance to your partner
YOUR_TAX_CODE_INCLUDES_OTHER_CALCULATION: Your tax code includes other calculations to work out your tax code allowances
YOUR_TAX_BASED_ON_PAY_PERIOD_NOT_WHOLE_Y: Your tax is based on your pay in each pay period, not the whole year. This stops you paying too much tax in one go.
YOUR_PENSION_COULD_SHOW_WEEK_MONTH_ONE: Your pension statement could show this as W1/M1 or week1/month1.
YOUR_PAYSLIP_COULD_SHOW_WEEK_MONTH_ONE: Your payslip could show this as W1/M1 or week1/month1.
YOUR_PERSONAL_ALLOWANCE_HAS_BEEN_USED_UP: Your Personal Allowance has been used up
YOUR_SCOTLAND_PERSONAL_ALLOWANCE_USED_UP: Your Personal Allowance (Scotland) has been used up
YOUR_WALES_PERSONAL_ALLOWANCE_USED_UP: Your Personal Allowance (Wales) has been used up
YOUR_TAX_FREE_AMOUNT_FOR_THIS_EMPLOYMENT: Your tax-free amount for this employment or pension is
FOR_SECURITY_WE_SIGNED_YOU_OUT: For your security, we signed you out
IF_THIS_CHANGES_TAX_LIABLE_PAY: If this changes the amount of tax you are liable to pay, we will adjust your tax-free allowance. If we need to, we will update your tax code to make sure you are paying the correct amount of tax.
YOU_DO_NOT_HAVE_TO_DO_ANYTHING: You do not have to do anything.
WHAT_IS_BENEFIT_IN_KIND: What is a benefit in kind?
WHEN_MAKING_GROSS_PENSIONAL_PAYMENTS: When making gross Personal Pension Payments, you are automatically given tax relief at the basic rate of 20% by your pension company.
CUSTOMERS_LIVING_IN_UK_PAY_TAX: Customers living in the UK who pay tax at above the basic rate are due a further 20% tax relief, you will only receive the further relief on the amount of higher rate tax you have paid.
CUSTOMERS_LIVING_IN_SCOTLAND_DIFFERENT_L: Customers living in Scotland have different relief levels.
WE_INCLUDE_TAX_FREE_ADDITIONAL: We may include an additional tax-free allowance into your tax code to allow the extra tax relief you are due on your Personal Pension Payments.
YOU_CAN_FIND_MORE_INFO_IN: You can find more information in
THE_GUIDANCE_ON_TAX_PRIVATE_PENSION: the guidance on tax on your private pension contributions (opens in a new tab)
AS_EMPLOYEE_COMPANY_BENEFITS_ACOMMODATIO: As an employee, you pay tax on company benefits like accommodation and loans and your employer takes the tax you owe from your wages through Pay As You Earn (PAYE).
THE_AMOUNT_PAY_DEPENDS_KIND_BENEFITS: The amount you pay depends on what kind of benefits you get and their value which your employer works out. Some company benefits can be tax-free, like childcare and canteen meals.
THE_GUIDANCE_ON_TAX_COMPANY_BENEFITS: the guidance on the tax on company benefits (opens in new tab)
YOU_MAY_GET_A_DIVIDEND_PAYMENT_SHARES_IN: You may get a dividend payment if you own shares in a company. You can earn some dividend income each year without paying tax. You do not pay tax on any dividend income that falls within your Personal Allowance.
YOU_ALSO_GET_DIVIDEND_ALLOWANCE_EACH_YEA: You also get a dividend allowance each year and only pay tax on any dividend income above the dividend allowance. The amount of tax you pay on dividends above the dividend allowance depends on your Income Tax band.
WE_HAVE_INCLUDED_AN_ADJUSTMENT_TAX_CODE_: We have included an adjustment in your tax code to collect basic rate tax on your dividend income.
THE_GUIDANCE_ON_TAX_DIVIDENDS: the guidance on the tax on dividends (opens in a new tab)
YOUC_CAN_FIND_MORE_INFO_DIVIDENDS: You can find more information in
WE_HAVE_ADJUSTED_YOUR_TAX_FREE_ALLOWANCE: We have adjusted your tax-free allowance by including a higher rate adjustment in your tax code to collect the additional tax due on your investments such as dividends.
THIS_INCOME_TAXABLE_DO_NOT_TAKE_OFF_PAYM: This income is taxable but we do not take tax off the payments before you receive them.
TO_ENUSRE_PAYING_CORRECT_AMOUNT_TAX: To ensure you are paying the correct amount of tax, we will reduce your tax-free allowance.
FOR_MORE_INFO_ON_HOW_STATE_PENSION: For more information on how the State Pension can affect the tax you pay,
WATCH_OUR_VIDEO: watch our Youtube video (opens in new tab)
WE_WILL_UPDATE_YOUR_TAX_CODE_SO_YOU_PAY_: We will update your tax code so you will only pay tax on the amount you are entitled to.
WE_DO_NOT_CHARGE_TAX_CODE_CASUAL: We do not change your tax code for these casual earnings.
AFTER_CHECK_YOU_PAID_RIGHT_TAX_WE_SEND_C: After we check if you have paid the right amount of tax at the end of the tax year, we will send you a tax calculation letter. This will include any casual earnings.
IF_YOU_FILE_SA_TAX_RETURN_CASUAL_EARNING: If you file a Self Assessment tax return you should include your casual earnings on the return.
THIS_IS_LUMP_SUM_PAID_WHEN_YOU_DECIDE_TA: This is a lump sum paid as you reached State Pension age before 6 April 2016, deferred receipt of your State Pension for at least 12 months and chose to take a lump sum when you decided to start taking your State Pension.
SPECIAL_RULES_APPLIED_WHEN_CALCULATE_LUM: Special rules are applied when we calculate any tax due on this lump sum. The lump sum itself is ignored when we work out which tax band your income falls into but any normal State Pension payments you are entitled to will be included.
YOU_WILL_BE_TAXED_YOUR_HIGHEST_RATE_LIAB: You will be taxed at your highest rate of tax you are liable for however the 0% tax rates applied to savings interest and dividend income are ignored.
IF_INCOME_EXCEEDS_PERSONAL_ALLOWANCE_LUM: If the income which exceeds your Personal Allowance is all taxed at 0%, you would be liable to pay 20% tax on the State Pension lump sum.
DIFFERENT_RATES_APPLY_IF_SCOTTISH: Different rates apply if you are a Scottish resident in the tax year the lump sum is taxed in.
WE_ALSO_TAKE_ACCOUNT_DWP: We will also take into account any tax the Department for Work and Pensions (DWP) deducted from the lump sum when they paid it.
THIS_IS_AMOUNT_PERSONAL_ALLOWANCE_CIVIL_: This is the amount of Personal Allowance that you have transferred to your spouse or civil partner through Marriage Allowance.
THIS_GIVES_SPOUSE_CIVIL_PARTNER_TAX_CRED: This gives your spouse or civil partner a tax credit of up to £252, which reduces the amount of tax they have to pay.
WE_WILL_ADJUST_TAX_FREE_ALLOWANCE_MAKE_S: We will adjust your tax-free allowance by updating your tax code to make sure you are paying the correct tax.
YOUR_UPDATE_TAX_CODE_USUALLY_INCLUDE_LET: Your updated tax code will usually include the letter 'N' as you are transferring part of your Personal Allowance to your spouse or civil partner.
THIS_PERSONAL_ALLOWANCE_SPOUSE_CIVIL_PAR: This is the amount of Personal Allowance that your spouse or civil partner has transferred to you through Marriage Allowance.
THIS_GIVES_TAX_CREDIT_REDUCES_TAX_AMOUNT: This gives you a tax credit of up to £252, which reduces the amount of tax you have to pay.
WHEN_WE_REVIEW_YOUR_TAX_POSITION_GET_PER: When we review your tax position after the tax year ends, you will still get your full Personal Allowance and up to £252 tax credit.
YOUR_UPDATE_TAX_CODE_INCLUDE_M: Your updated tax code will usually include the letter 'M' as you are receiving part of your spouse or civil partner's Personal Allowance.
WE_CALCULATE_SURPLUS_BLIND_PERSONS_ALLOW: We have calculated that there is surplus Blind Person's Allowance available to your spouse or civil partner.
IF_NECESSARY_WE_UPDATE_BLIND_PERSONS_ALL: If necessary we will update their Income Tax records to reflect this but you will remain entitled to the full Blind Person's Allowance if your actual income for the year means you need this.
SO_THAT_YOU_NOT_PAY_TAX_UNNECESSARILY: So that you do not pay tax unnecessarily, we will not change the Blind Person's Allowance included in your tax code.
WHAT_ARE_TOTAL_GIFT_AID_PAYMENTS: What are total Gift Aid payments?
WHEN_MAKING_GIFT_AID_PAYMENTS: When making Gift Aid payments to registered charities or amateur sports clubs and you pay tax at rates above basic rate you may be entitled to further tax relief.
CUSTOMERS_LIVING_IN_UK_WHO_PAY_TAX: Customers living in the UK who pay tax at above the basic rate are due a further 20% tax relief, you will only receive the further relief on the amount of higher rate tax you have paid.
CUSTOMERS_LIVING_IN_SCOTLAND: Customers living in Scotland have different relief levels.
WE_HAVE_INCLUDED_ADDITIONAL_TAX_FREE_ALL: We have included an additional tax-free allowance into your tax code to allow the extra tax relief you are due on your charitable donations.
GUIDANCE_ON_DONATE_TO_CHARITY: the guidance on tax relief when you donate to a charity (opens in new tab)
WHAT_ARE_GIFT_AID_PAYMENTS: What are total Gift Aid payments?
WE_HAVE_CALCULATE_SURPLUS_BLIND_PERSONS_: We have calculated that there is surplus Blind Person's Allowance available to your spouse or civil partner.
IF_NECESSARY_WILL_UPDATE_INCOME_TAX: If necessary we will update their Income Tax records to reflect this but you will remain entitled to the full Blind Person's Allowance if your actual income for the year means you need this.
SO_THAT_YOU_NOT_PAY_TAX: So that you do not pay tax unnecessarily, we will not change the Blind Person's Allowance included in your tax code.
WHAT_IS_BPA_TRANSFERRED: What is surplus Blind Person's Allowance transferred to spouse or civil partner?
WHERE_YOU_UNUSED_BPA: Where you have unused Blind Person's Allowance this can be transferred to a spouse or civil partner.
BECAUSE_YOU_ALWAYS_ENTITLED_BPA: Because you are always entitled to use the full amount of Blind Person's Allowance we will not change the amount of Blind Person's Allowance included in your tax code but we will update your spouse or civil partners tax code where necessary.
IF_THIS_CHANGE_TX_AMOUNT_ADJUST_LIABLE: If this changes the amount of tax you are liable to pay, we will adjust your tax-free allowance.
IF_WE_NEED_WE_UPDATE_MAKE_PAYING: If we need to, we will update your tax code to make sure you're paying the correct amount of tax.
WHAT_IS_BPA_RECEIVED: What is surplus Blind Person's Allowance received from spouse or civil partner?
NON_CODED_INCOME_EARNINGS_NOT_INCLUDED: Non-coded income is earnings or income that is not included in your tax code.
IF_THIS_CHANGES_AMOUNT_TAX_LIABLE_PAY: If this changes the amount of tax you are liable to pay, we will adjust your tax-free allowance.
IF_WE_NEED_WE_UPDATE_TAX_CODE: If we need to, we will update your tax code to make sure you're paying the correct amount of tax.
BPA_TRANSFERED_FROM_CLAIMANT: Surplus Blind Person’s Allowance can be transferred from the claimant to their spouse or civil partner where the claimant has some Blind Person’s Allowance that they cannot use.
WE_HAVE_CALCULATED_BPA_AVAILABLE: We have calculated that there is surplus Blind Person’s Allowance available to you and have updated your Income Tax record to reflect this. This can reduce the amount of income you have to pay tax on.

THIS_IS_TO_RECOVER_CHB: This is to recover the Child Benefit you or your partner receives as we expect your income to exceed £60,000.
YOU_CAN_FIND_MORE_INFO_CHB: You can find more information in
THE_GUIDANCE_ON_HIGH_INCOME_CHILD_BENEFI: the guidance on the High Income Child Benefit Charge (opens in new tab)
IF_THIS_CHANGE_TX_AMOUNT_ADJUST_LIABLE_D: If this changes the amount of tax you are liable to pay, we will adjust your tax-free allowance.
IF_WE_NEED_UPDATE_TAX_CODE: If we need to, we will update your tax code to make sure you are paying the correct amount of tax.
IF_YOU_ARE_LIABLE_HICBIC: If you are liable for the High Income Child Benefit Charge and this changes the amount of tax you are liable to pay, we will adjust your tax-free allowance.

THE_GUIDANCE_HICBIC_CATC1: the guidance on High Income Child Benefit Charge (opens in new tab)
EXPLAINER_CAT_C: If this changes the amount of tax you are liable to pay, we will adjust your tax-free allowance. If we need to, we will update your tax code to make sure you are paying the correct amount of tax.

SPECIAL_TAX.BR_EMPLOYER: This code means your employer will take 20% tax (the basic rate) from all your pay from this employment.
SPECIAL_TAX.D0_EMPLOYER: This code means your employer will take 40% tax (the higher rate) from all your pay from this employment.
SPECIAL_TAX.D1_EMPLOYER: This code means your employer will take 45% tax (the additional rate) from all your pay from this employment.
SPECIAL_TAX.SBR_EMPLOYER: This code means your employer will take 20% tax (the basic rate in Scotland) from all your pay from this employment.
SPECIAL_TAX.SD0_EMPLOYER: This code means your employer will take 21% tax (the intermediate rate in Scotland) from all your pay from this employment.
SPECIAL_TAX.SD1_EMPLOYER: This code means your employer will take 42% tax (the higher rate in Scotland) from all your pay from this employment.
SPECIAL_TAX.SD2_EMPLOYER: This code means your employer will take 45% tax (the advanced rate in Scotland) from all your pay from this employment.
SPECIAL_TAX.SD3_EMPLOYER: This code means your employer will take 48% tax (the top rate in Scotland) from all your pay from this employment.
SPECIAL_TAX.CBR_EMPLOYER: This code means your employer will take 20% tax (the basic rate in Wales) from all your pay from this employment.
SPECIAL_TAX.CD0_EMPLOYER: This code means your employer will take 40% tax (the higher rate in Wales) from all your pay from this employment.
SPECIAL_TAX.CD1_EMPLOYER: This code means your employer will take 45% tax (the additional rate in Wales) from all your pay from this employment.
SPECIAL_TAX.NT_EMPLOYER: This code means you’re not paying any tax on income from this employment.
SPECIAL_TAX.BR_PENSION: This code means your pension provider will take 20% tax (the basic rate) from all your income from this pension.
SPECIAL_TAX.D0_PENSION: This code means your pension provider will take 40% tax (the higher rate) from all your income from this pension.
SPECIAL_TAX.D1_PENSION: This code means your pension provider will take 45% tax (the additional rate) from all your income from this pension.
SPECIAL_TAX.SBR_PENSION: This code means your pension provider will take 20% tax (the basic rate in Scotland) from all your income from this pension.
SPECIAL_TAX.SD0_PENSION: This code means your pension provider will take 21% tax (the intermediate rate in Scotland) from all your income from this pension.
SPECIAL_TAX.SD1_PENSION: This code means your pension provider will take 42% tax (the higher rate in Scotland) from all your income from this pension.
SPECIAL_TAX.SD2_PENSION: This code means your pension provider will take 45% tax (the advanced rate in Scotland) from all your income from this pension.
SPECIAL_TAX.SD3_PENSION: This code means your pension provider will take 48% tax (the top rate in Scotland) from all your income from this pension.
SPECIAL_TAX.CBR_PENSION: This code means your pension provider will take 20% tax (the basic rate in Wales) from all your income from this pension.
SPECIAL_TAX.CD0_PENSION: This code means your pension provider will take 40% tax (the higher rate in Wales) from all your income from this pension.
SPECIAL_TAX.CD1_PENSION: This code means your pension provider will take 45% tax (the additional rate in Wales) from all your income from this pension.
SPECIAL_TAX.NT_PENSION: This code means you’re not paying any tax on income from this pension.
SPECIAL_TAX.WE_USE_THIS_CODE_EMPLOYER: We use this code because you have more than one employment or pension, and your tax-free amount is used at another employment or pension.
SPECIAL_TAX.WE_USE_THIS_CODE_PENSION: We use this code because you have more than one pension or employment, and your tax-free amount is used at another pension or employment.

UNTAXED_SAVINGS_INTEREST.UNTAXED_SAVINGS_INTEREST_LINK_DESCRIPTIO: This is interest you received without tax taken off, usually from your bank or building society.
UNTAXED_SAVINGS_INTEREST.DESCRIPTION_1: Untaxed savings interest is interest that has not had tax taken off before you receive it. It usually comes from standard bank or building society savings accounts. It does not include interest from Cash ISAs, which are tax-free.
UNTAXED_SAVINGS_INTEREST.DESCRIPTION_2: Each year, your bank or building society tells HMRC how much interest you earned.
UNTAXED_SAVINGS_INTEREST.WHY_IN_TAX_CODE: Why it’s in your tax code
UNTAXED_SAVINGS_INTEREST.WHY_IN_TAX_CODE_DESCRIPTION: HMRC uses the figures sent by your bank or building society to help make sure you pay the right amount of tax.
UNTAXED_SAVINGS_INTEREST.SELF_ASSESSMENT: If you send a Self Assessment return
UNTAXED_SAVINGS_INTEREST.NO_SELF_ASSESSMENT: If you do not send a Self Assessment return
UNTAXED_SAVINGS_INTEREST.PERSONAL_SAVINGS_ALLOWANCE_DESCRIPTION_1: Most people can earn some savings interest without paying tax. This tax-free amount is called the Personal Savings Allowance.
UNTAXED_SAVINGS_INTEREST.PERSONAL_SAVINGS_ALLOWANCE_DESCRIPTION_2: Savings interest is taxed using the UK-wide basic, higher and additional rate bands. These bands apply in the same way whether you live in England, Scotland, Wales or Northern Ireland.
UNTAXED_SAVINGS_INTEREST.ALLOWANCE_BASIC_RATE: up to £1,000 if your overall income puts you in the basic rate band
UNTAXED_SAVINGS_INTEREST.ALLOWANCE_HIGHER_RATE: up to £500 if your overall income puts you in the higher rate band
UNTAXED_SAVINGS_INTEREST.ALLOWANCE_ADDITIONAL_RATE: £0 if your overall income puts you in the additional rate band
UNTAXED_SAVINGS_INTEREST.ALLOWANCE_TAX_CODE: We take your allowance into account when we work out your tax code.
UNTAXED_SAVINGS_INTEREST.FIGURE_MISMATCH: Why the figure in your tax code may not match your bank statement
UNTAXED_SAVINGS_INTEREST.FIGURE_MISMATCH_DESCRIPTION: The untaxed interest shown in your tax code can differ from the amount on your bank statements because:
UNTAXED_SAVINGS_INTEREST.MISMATCH_REASON_1: the first part of your interest is covered by your Personal Savings Allowance
UNTAXED_SAVINGS_INTEREST.MISMATCH_REASON_2: only the taxable part of your interest is included in the code
UNTAXED_SAVINGS_INTEREST.MISMATCH_REASON_3: the code may contain an estimate based on the latest information we have
UNTAXED_SAVINGS_INTEREST.MISMATCH_REASON_4: if you usually pay no tax, we may include some savings interest to use up any spare allowances
UNTAXED_SAVINGS_INTEREST.MORE_HELP_LINK_TEXT: GOV.UK Tax on savings interest (opens in new tab)
UNTAXED_SAVINGS_INTEREST.UNTAXED_SAVINGS_INTEREST_CHECK: We check your savings interest after the end of the tax year and contact you if you owe extra tax or are due a refund.
UNTAXED_SAVINGS_INTEREST.INCLUDE_INTEREST_RECEIVED: Include the interest you received. We normally use the amount you report to keep your tax code up to date unless you ask us not to.

YOU_CAN_NOW_SEE_HOW_MUCH_TAX_YOU_WILL_PA: You can now view how much tax you will pay from
AND_COMPARE_IT_WITH_WHAT_YOU_PAY_THIS_YE: and compare it with what you pay this year.
KEEPING_YOUR_PAY_AND_PENSION_DETAILS_UP_: Keeping your pay and pension details up to date helps make sure your tax is right for the new tax year.
CHECK_THE_FIGURES_WE_HOLD_FOR_YOU: Check the figures we hold for you
CHECK_THE_FIGURES_LOOK_RIGHT: check the figures look right – if they do, you do not need to do anything
UPDATE_YOUR_ESTIMATED_INCOME_IF_IT_HAS_C: update your estimated income if it has changed
TELL_US_ABOUT_CHANGES_TO_COMPANY_BENEFIT: tell us about changes to company benefits, allowances or tax reliefs, or income from other sources
PAYMENT_TO_HELP_WHC: payment to help with winter heating costs.
THIS_IS_WFP_BRITAIN_PENSION_SCOTLAND: This is called the Winter Fuel Payment if you live in England, Wales or Northern Ireland, or the Pension Age Winter Heating Payment if you live in Scotland.
IT_PAID_BY_DWP: It is paid by the Department for Work and Pensions (DWP) or Social Security Scotland.
YOU_NEED_PAY_THIS_TAX_CODE: , you need to pay this back through your tax code.
TO_COLLECT_OWE_ADJUSTED_YOUR_TAX_CODE: To collect what you owe, we have adjusted your tax code.
THIS_MEANS_YOU_WILL_PAY_MORE_TAX: This means you'll pay a little more tax through your pay or pension to cover this amount.
YOU_DONT_CONTACT_SEPARATE_PAYEMENT: You do not need to contact us, and you cannot make a separate payment. We'll collect the amount automatically.
WE_ADJUSTED_ALLOWANCES_BY_LARGER_AMOUNT: We need to adjust your tax allowances by a larger amount than the payment you received so more of your income is taxed.
IN_TAX_OVER_COVER_MONEY_WFP: in tax over the year to cover the money you received in the Winter Fuel Payment or Pension Age Winter Heating Payment.
EXAMPLE_WFP_TAX_CODE: Example: How we collect Winter Fuel Payment or Pension Age Winter Heating Payment through a tax code
IF_SOMENONE_GET_WFP_PA_WHP: If someone gets a £200 Winter Fuel Payment or Pension Age Winter Heating Payment and pays tax at 20%, we collect the £200 by changing their tax code.
TO_COLLECT_TAX_RATE: To collect £200 at a 20% tax rate:
WE_REDUCE_THEIR_TA: we reduce their tax allowances by £1,000
THIS_MEANS_THEIR_INCOME_IS_TAXED: this means £1,000 more of their income is taxed
THEY_WILL_PAY_TAX_OVER_YEAR: they will pay £200 more tax over the year
WE_WILL_COLLECT_AMOUNT_DURING_NEXT_TAX_Y: We'll collect the amount during the next tax year
IT_MAY_BE_SHOWN_IN_YOUR_TAX_CODE: It may be shown in your tax code as an underpayment from a previous year.
IF_INCOME_FOR_TAX_YEAR_YOU_DO_NOT_OWE: If your income for the tax year is
LESS_AND_DO_NOT_OWE: or less and you do not owe anything, we'll update your tax code automatically.
YOU_DO_NOT_NEED_TO_CONTACT_US: You do not need to contact us.
WINTER_FUEL_PAYMENT_OR_PENSION_AGE_WINTE: Winter Fuel Payment or Pension Age Winter Heating Payment
THE_WINTER_FUEL_PAYMENT_IS_A_YEARLY_PAYM: The Winter Fuel Payment is a yearly payment to help eligible people with their heating costs.
IF_YOU_LIVE_IN_SCOTLAND: If you live in Scotland, this is called the Pension Age Winter Heating Payment.
YOU_NORMALLY_DO_NOT_NEED_TO_APPLY: You normally do not need to apply for a Winter Fuel Payment or Pension Age Winter Heating Payment.
MOST_PEOPLE_GET_IT_AUTOMATICALLY: Most people get it automatically if they're eligible.
FIND_OUT_MORE: Find out more about Winter Fuel Payments (opens in a new tab)
HOW_IT_COULD_AFFECT_YOUR_TAX: How Winter Fuel Payment or Pension Age Winter Heating Payment could affect your tax
AFFECTS_TAX_CODE_NOT_INCOME: Your Winter Fuel Payment or Pension Age Winter Heating Payment affects your tax code, not your taxable income.
HOW_IT_AFFECTS_YOU: How it affects you depends on your total income for the tax year.
NO_EFFECT_ON_TAX_PAID: The Winter Fuel Payment or Pension Age Winter Heating Payment does not affect the amount of tax you pay.
IF_TOTAL_INCOME_IS_OVER: If your total income is over
PAY_BACK_PAYMENT: You will need to pay back the Winter Fuel Payment or Pension Age Winter Heating Payment. We will recover the amount automatically through your tax code or tax return.
NO_NEED_TO_CONTACT: You do not need to contact us or make a separate payment.
IF_SELF_ASSESSMENT: If you complete a Self Assessment tax return
PAYE_SOURCE_NOTE: If you pay tax through Self Assessment and have a PAYE source, your tax code might not change.
RECOVER_THROUGH_SELF_ASSESSMENT: We will recover the amount through your Self Assessment calculation.
SAME_TAX_RULES_APPLY: If you get Pension Age Winter Heating Payment, the same tax rules apply.
WE_ARE_CHANGING_YOUR_TAX_CODE_FOR: We’re changing your tax code for
BASED_ON_CURRENT_INCOME: Based on your current income we estimate that you’ll pay
THIS_AMOUNT_IS_AN_ESTIMATE: This amount is an estimate because your earnings could change in the future.
NO_ACTION_NEEDED_EMPLOYMENT_AT: You do not need to take any action. We’ll automatically collect the tax through your employment at
THIS_MEANS_TAX_CODE_WILL_CHANGE_ACCORDIN: This means your tax code will change accordingly.
NO_ACTION_NEEDED_VIA_PENSION_OR_PAY_AT: You do not need to take any action. We’ll automatically collect the tax through your
THIS_MEANS_TAX_CODE_WILL_CHANGE_FROM_TO: This means your tax code will change from
CHECK_HOW_WE_WORKED_OUT_YOUR_NEW_CODE_AN: Check how we worked out your new code and tax-free amount
THIS_IS_FOR_INFO_ONLY_NOT_DO_ANYTHING_UN: This is for your information only. You do not have to do anything, unless you need to tell us something has changed.
